title: China Solar Exports Hit Monthly Record 68 GW in March — 50% Above Prior Record
detail: Amid persistently high global oil prices driven by Middle East tensions, nations accelerated their clean energy procurement in early 2026. Ember energy analyst data reported by CNN on April 26 revealed China exported 68 GW worth of solar technology in March 2026 — a new monthly record that exceeded the previous record (set in August 2025) by 50%. China is expected to commission 390 GW of new solar PV and 86 GW of new wind in 2026 — representing approximately 56% and 60% of new world solar and wind capacity respectively. The surge reflects both domestic installation rates and global demand for Chinese-manufactured clean energy equipment as countries seek to reduce fossil fuel import dependence. Analysts noted the energy security dynamic is now reinforcing climate investment, with European households buying record numbers of EVs, heat pumps, and rooftop solar panels.

media.caption: China exported 68 GW of solar technology in March 2026, a monthly record driven by global clean energy demand surge

title: Oxfam: Six Fossil Fuel Giants Projected to Earn $2,967/Second in 2026; Majority Back Windfall Tax for Clean Energy
detail: Oxfam released a major report on April 26 showing six major fossil fuel companies are projected to earn approximately $2,967 per second in profits in 2026, totaling tens of billions annually. The report cited polling showing three times as many citizens globally support investment in renewable energy over increased fossil fuel extraction, and that 68% of respondents support windfall taxes on fossil fuel profits to fund the clean energy transition. The report was released two days before the high-level phase of the First International Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels in Santa Marta, Colombia (April 28-29), adding political momentum to the summit's push for legally binding fossil fuel phase-out instruments and transition finance mechanisms.

media.caption: Oxfam report shows fossil fuel giants earn nearly $3,000/second in 2026 as 68% of global citizens back windfall taxes for clean energy
